messages.content: You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What are common signs of dehydration?

Common signs include increased thirst, dry mouth, dark yellow urine, reduced urination, fatigue, dizziness, and headache. Severe dehydration can cause confusion or a rapid heartbeat and needs urgent care. Drink fluids and seek medical help if symptoms are severe or persist.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is the difference between a viral and a bacterial infection?

Viral infections are caused by viruses and usually resolve on their own; antibiotics do not work against them. Bacterial infections are caused by bacteria and may be treated with antibiotics when appropriate. A clinician can order tests to tell them apart, so consult a professional for a proper diagnosis.

What is a normal resting blood pressure range for adults?

A typical normal reading is around 120/80 mmHg. Readings consistently at or above 130/80 mmHg may indicate high blood pressure. Blood pressure varies, so have it measured by a healthcare professional for an accurate assessment.

How should I care for a minor burn at home?

Cool the burn under clean running water for about 20 minutes, remove tight items nearby, and cover it loosely with a sterile non-stick dressing. Do not apply ice, butter, or pop blisters. Seek medical care for large, deep, or chemical burns, or burns on the face, hands, or genitals.

What does BMI measure?

Body Mass Index estimates body fat from weight relative to height, calculated as weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared. It is a general screening tool and does not account for muscle mass or fat distribution, so discuss your results with a healthcare professional.

When is chest pain a medical emergency?

Chest pain with shortness of breath, sweating, nausea, or pain spreading to the arm, jaw, or back can signal a heart attack and is a medical emergency. Call emergency services immediately rather than waiting. When in doubt, treat sudden severe chest pain as urgent.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is type 2 diabetes?

Type 2 diabetes is a condition where the body does not use insulin effectively, leading to high blood sugar over time. It is managed with lifestyle changes, monitoring, and sometimes medication. A healthcare professional can confirm the diagnosis and create a treatment plan.

Why is hand hygiene important?

Washing hands with soap and water removes germs and helps prevent the spread of infections like colds, flu, and gastrointestinal illness. Wash for at least 20 seconds, especially before eating and after using the restroom. Use hand sanitizer when soap is unavailable.

What does a complete blood count test measure?

A complete blood count measures red blood cells, white blood cells, hemoglobin, hematocrit, and platelets. It helps screen for conditions such as anemia, infection, and clotting problems. Your clinician interprets the results in the context of your overall health.

How much sleep do adults generally need?

Most adults need about seven to nine hours of sleep per night for good health. Consistent, sufficient sleep supports memory, mood, and immune function. If you regularly struggle with sleep, talk with a healthcare professional.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
Do antibiotics treat the flu?

No. The flu is caused by a virus, and antibiotics only work against bacteria. Taking antibiotics unnecessarily can cause side effects and contribute to antibiotic resistance. Rest, fluids, and antiviral medication when prescribed are usually the right approach.

What are common symptoms of seasonal allergies?

Seasonal allergies often cause sneezing, a runny or stuffy nose, itchy or watery eyes, and an itchy throat. Triggers include pollen from trees, grasses, and weeds. Avoiding triggers and using recommended antihistamines can help; see a professional if symptoms are severe.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What does vaccination do?

Vaccination helps your immune system recognize and fight a specific germ without causing the full disease. Vaccines reduce your risk of serious illness and can protect people around you by lowering spread. For individual questions, talk with a healthcare professional.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is hypertension?

Hypertension, or high blood pressure, means blood pushes harder than normal against artery walls. It often has no symptoms but can increase the risk of heart disease and stroke. A healthcare professional can measure it and recommend lifestyle changes or treatment.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is the difference between a symptom and a diagnosis?

A symptom is what a person feels, like pain or nausea. A diagnosis is a clinician’s conclusion about the underlying condition causing those symptoms. Share symptoms with a licensed healthcare professional for proper evaluation.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is a preventive health exam?

A preventive health exam is a routine visit focused on screening and health counseling before you feel sick. It may include checks like blood pressure, vaccines, and advice on diet and exercise. Your doctor can recommend which preventive services are right for you.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
When should I see a doctor for a fever?

See a doctor if a fever is very high, lasts more than a couple of days, or comes with serious symptoms like difficulty breathing, confusion, rash, or severe pain. Young children, older adults, and people with weakened immune systems may need care sooner.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is mental health self-care?

Mental health self-care means using habits like sleep, exercise, social connection, and stress management to support emotional well-being. It is not a substitute for professional care when needed, but it can help maintain resilience. If you are struggling, reach out to a licensed mental health provider.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is the difference between a side effect and an allergy?

A side effect is a predictable reaction that can happen with many medications, like nausea or drowsiness. An allergy is an immune response that can cause hives, swelling, or breathing problems and may be serious. Tell your healthcare provider about any reactions so they can guide you safely.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is a healthy eating pattern?

A healthy eating pattern includes vegetables, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats while limiting excess sugar, salt, and highly processed foods. Drinking enough water and eating balanced portions also helps. A registered dietitian can give personalized advice.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is a chronic condition?

A chronic condition is a health issue that lasts a long time or recurs, such as diabetes, asthma, or arthritis. It often requires ongoing management rather than a one-time cure. Work with your healthcare provider to manage chronic conditions safely.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is the common cold?

The common cold is a mild viral infection of the nose and throat that causes symptoms like a runny nose, sore throat, and cough. It usually improves on its own in a few days. Rest, fluids, and over-the-counter relief can help.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is high blood sugar?

High blood sugar, or hyperglycemia, means there is too much glucose in the blood. It can occur in diabetes and may cause thirst, frequent urination, and fatigue. A healthcare professional can help manage it with diet, medication, and monitoring.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is telehealth?

Telehealth is receiving healthcare services remotely by phone or video instead of in person. It can be useful for follow-up visits, medication questions, and some types of care. Check with your provider to see what telehealth options are available.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is a vaccine booster?

A booster is an additional vaccine dose given after the initial series to strengthen or extend protection. Boosters may be recommended when immunity declines or when new variants appear. Follow public health guidance and your provider’s advice.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is asthma?

Asthma is a chronic condition where the airways become inflamed and narrow, causing wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. It can be managed with inhalers and an asthma action plan from a healthcare provider.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is a migraine?

A migraine is a headache disorder often featuring throbbing pain, sensitivity to light or sound, and nausea. It may last hours or days. A healthcare professional can help identify triggers and treatment options.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is a fever?

A fever is an elevated body temperature that often indicates the body is fighting an infection. It can be managed with rest, fluids, and fever reducers if needed. Seek medical advice if a fever is very high or persists.

What is a healthy eating pattern?

A healthy eating pattern includes vegetables, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats while limiting excess sugar and highly processed foods. Drinking enough water and eating balanced portions also helps. A registered dietitian can provide personalized guidance.

What is a chronic condition?

A chronic condition is a health issue that lasts a long time or recurs, such as diabetes, asthma, or arthritis. It often requires ongoing management rather than a one-time cure. Work with your healthcare provider to manage chronic conditions safely.

What is a headache?
A headache is pain or discomfort in the head or neck. It can have many causes, including tension, dehydration, or illness. If headaches are frequent or severe, see a healthcare professional.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is an electrolyte?

Electrolytes are minerals like sodium and potassium that help regulate fluid balance and muscle function. They are important for hydration and can be lost through sweat or illness. A healthcare provider can advise if you need to replace electrolytes.

You are a careful medical information assistant. Provide general, educational health information in plain language, and always recommend consulting a licensed healthcare professional for diagnosis or treatment.
What is stress management?

Stress management includes activities like exercise, relaxation, sleep, and time management to reduce stress levels. It can improve mood and overall health. If stress is overwhelming, talk with a healthcare professional or counselor.

See a doctor if a fever is very high, lasts more than a couple of days, or comes with serious symptoms like difficulty breathing, confusion, or rash. Young children, older adults, and people with weakened immune systems may need care sooner. Follow your provider’s advice for your situation.
